prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f3e14006`.
codex_response:
Implemented `f3e14006` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: find the densest nonzero row and column, use the row’s repeated accent color to set the horizontal span, use the column’s repeated accent color on one side of the intersection to set the vertical span, then rebuild that rectangle with alternating patterned and separator rows; near the intersection the patterned rows use the row accent, farther out they use the column accent, and the intersection color controls the odd-column filler on the separator rows and the special near-accent row. No new primitive was needed.

I used `arc2_opus46_summary.json` only as a starting hypothesis and corrected the hints in notes, because they missed the intersection-color behavior on the separator rows and the special near-accent patterned row. `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task f3e14006` passed `6/6` official examples, and `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task f3e14006 --n-examples 1000` produced the full artifact set. The generator matches the family by sampling a single near-full row and column, repeated accent pairs, both above/below column-accent layouts, left/right/straddle row spans, and both intersection-majority variants.
